strategies: pair each inference claim with the sample and design features that must be true for the claim to hold
compare matched studies that differ only in sampling method or design quality
require learners to restate a conclusion with explicit uncertainty and scope limits
compare random, convenience, and biased samples drawn from the same hypothetical population
use teacher think-alouds naming who is represented and who may be missing
require learners to explain why the sample should or should not generalize
compare surveys, observational studies, and experiments on the same topic and discuss claim limits
require learners to name what extra design feature would be needed to support a causal claim
use matched examples where a strong association still does not justify causation
use study-review routines that check sample selection, measurement method, and confounding factors one by one
compare stronger and weaker studies of the same question and identify the design weakness
require learners to name the most likely bias or weakness before accepting a conclusion
